pr.title: Remove unused unions in lb/types.h and it/types.h
pr.author: r-burns

summary: Removed unused anonymous union layers from item and library type headers, then updated item code to use the remaining direct fields and bitfield views. The cleanup converted Item::xDC0 from flag32 to u32, simplified flag32 to expose only its flags struct, flattened several lb/types.h bitfield wrappers, and adjusted call sites in item collision/behavior code. decomp.dev reported net matching improvements in main/melee/it/it_2725, especially it_80277040, it_802765BC, and it_802742F4, with one small regression in it_80276934.

change_classification.primary_type: header type cleanup with decomp-matching call-site adjustments

what_changed: In src/melee/it/types.h, struct flag32 no longer contains an anonymous union of flags and word; it now exposes only a nested flags bitfield struct.
In Item, xDA4 and xDA8 were simplified from anonymous unions to xDA4_word and xDA8_short, and xDC0 was changed from flag32 to u32.
In src/melee/lb/types.h, HitCapsule offset +43 bitfields were flattened by removing the union containing x43 and individual x43_b* bits.
In src/melee/lb/types.h, ECBFlagStruct was flattened by removing the raw byte union member and leaving direct b0, b1234, b5, b6, and b7 bitfields.
In src/melee/lb/types.h CollData, two float members x11C_f32 and x120_f32 were removed from the shown anonymous struct region.
Call sites that previously used xDC0.word now use xDC0 directly, including masks, OR assignments, and zeroing in it_802750E8 and itcoll.c.
Call sites that previously hand-shifted xDC8_word.word were changed to use named bitfield members such as xDC8_word.flags.xF and xDC8_word.flags.x17.

it_802742F4 was locally simplified: the cast on Item_8026849C(item_gobj) was removed, xDC8 flag update became xDC8_word.flags.xF |= 1 << ftLib_80086BE0(gobj), temporary HSD_JObj variables were removed, and HSD_JObjClearFlagsAll now receives HSD_JObjGetChild(item_gobj->hsd_obj).
it_80276408 was wrapped in #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop, indicating a matching-sensitive inlining control.
A stale commented-out flag32 cast line was removed from itchicorita.c.
smart_moves: The PR did not just remove union members in headers; it also updated all visible call sites that depended on the old .word access pattern, keeping the type cleanup consistent.
Replacing manual shifts and masks like ((word >> 0xD) & 0xF) with xDC8_word.flags.xF made the intended bitfield use clearer and appears to have improved matching in multiple it_2725 functions.
Changing Item::xDC0 to a plain u32 avoided carrying a flag32 abstraction where the code only needed whole-word mask operations.
The cleanup preserved named bitfield access for xDC8_word where subfields are actively used, rather than flattening everything indiscriminately.
The dont_inline pragma on it_80276408 was a targeted matching tactic, not a broad compiler-setting change.
decomp_lessons: Anonymous unions are useful only when both views are actively needed. If call sites only use one view, removing the unused alias can simplify headers and sometimes improve codegen/matching.
When a field is semantically a mask word, model it as u32 and write direct bitwise operations instead of forcing it through a flag wrapper.
When a field is semantically a packed bitfield with known subranges, named bitfields like flags.xF and flags.x17 can replace brittle shift/mask expressions and document intent.
Header cleanup can require small source-level rewrites in unrelated-looking item functions because anonymous union member names leak into call-site syntax.
Matching improvements may come from both type-shape changes and expression-shape changes; the bot report credited improvements in it_80277040, it_802765BC, and it_802742F4 after these edits.

Use function helpers such as HSD_JObjGetChild when the helper expresses the target access pattern better than manually declaring temporaries, but verify exact behavior because the diff excerpt alone does not explain the prior null-check shape.
naming_conventions: Item bitfield submembers use x-prefixed hexadecimal/bit-offset style names such as x0, xF, x13, x17, and x1A.
Whole-word item fields retain suffixes describing the access width or role, such as xDA4_word, xDA8_short, xDC0, xDC4, and xDC8_word.
HitCapsule byte +43 bitfields use offset-plus-bit names x43_b0 through x43_b7.
ECBFlagStruct bitfields use compact bit names b0, b1234, b5, b6, and b7.
Unmatched item functions keep address-style names such as it_802742F4, it_802765BC, it_80276934, and it_80277040.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use named bitfield writes to preserve compact flag updates, e.g. xDC8_word.flags.xF |= 1 << ftLib_80086BE0(gobj), instead of reconstructing shifted whole-word expressions.
Use named bitfield reads for packed fields, e.g. xDC8_word.flags.x17, instead of (word >> 7U) & 3.
Use direct u32 mask operations for Item::xDC0 after converting it away from flag32, e.g. xDC0 |= 1, xDC0 |= 2, xDC0 = 0, and return xDC0 & mask.
Apply #pragma dont_inline locally around matching-sensitive helpers such as it_80276408 rather than changing broader compilation behavior.

Minor source expression changes, such as removing unnecessary casts or using truthiness checks for floats, were part of the diff and correlated with improved unmatched-function percentages, though the exact causal relationship is not proven by the available evidence.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.

decomp.dev bot reported three unmatched-function improvements in main/melee/it/it_2725: it_80277040 improved from 50.21% to 80.19% (+249 bytes), it_802765BC from 38.81% to 60.59% (+194 bytes), and it_802742F4 from 87.00% to 95.00% (+32 bytes).
decomp.dev bot reported one small regression: it_80276934 in main/melee/it/it_2725 moved from 61.02% to 60.59% (-4 bytes).
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching flag32 or xDC8_word to see whether more whole-word aliases were removed or restored.
Check whether the it_80276934 -4 byte regression was later recovered.
Search for remaining .word accesses on bitfield wrapper structs after this cleanup.
Audit other anonymous unions in item and library headers where only one view is used.
Compare CollData layout changes around x11C_f32 and x120_f32 with later struct-size or offset corrections.
